chunks.text:
The CLI ships in the opendetail package. Prefer npx, bunx, or pnpm dlx so you always run the version pinned by your project.

The opendetail package exposes build (index your docs), setup (scaffold config and route), and doctor (verify the setup).
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Install and first run

If that prints a version, the executable resolves correctly from your package manager.

Running the CLI without a subcommand prints supported commands and example invocations (same sections below).
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Typical flows

Greenfield app: opendetail setup to write opendetail.toml, scaffold the default route (self-hosted), and optionally run an initial index build.Docs changed: opendetail build to refresh .opendetail/index.json.Something looks wrong: opendetail doctor to verify config, index, route, and environment expectations.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Command overview

| Command | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| opendetail build | Write .opendetail/index.json from your config and content globs. |
| opendetail setup | Create opendetail.toml, optional route, optional first build. |
| opendetail doctor | Check files and env for the selected integration mode. |
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
One-liner usage

`opendetail build [--config <path>] [--output <path>] [--cwd <path>]
opendetail setup [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--base-path <url>] [--include <glob>] [--media-include <glob>] [--with-media] [--skip-build] [--force] [--interactive] [--no-interactive] [--cwd <path>]
opendetail doctor [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Suggested order

setup once per repo (or when you change integration mode).build whenever docs or media globs change.doctor when something fails or after upgrading packages.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
build

opendetail build is the index build step: it resolves paths from your config, walks the include globs, and writes .opendetail/index.json (or --output).
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Usage

`opendetail build [--config <path>] [--output <path>] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Flags

| Flag | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| --config | Path to opendetail.toml (default: ./opendetail.toml). |
| --output | Where to write the index JSON (default: .opendetail/index.json relative to cwd). |
| --cwd | Working directory for resolving paths. |
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: Examples
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
setup

opendetail setup writes the standard project shape: config, Next.js route, and initial index build.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Usage

`opendetail setup [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--base-path <url>] [--include <glob>] [--media-include <glob>] [--with-media] [--skip-build] [--force] [--interactive] [--no-interactive] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
What it can write

opendetail.tomlIn self-hosted mode, a route at src/app/api/opendetail/route.ts by default (override with --route).opendetail/index.json unless you pass --skip-build
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Important flags

| Flag | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| --config | Custom config path. |
| --route | Custom route file path (self-hosted). |
| --base-path | Public source URL base (default /docs). |
| --include | Docs glob (default matches typical content/**/*.{md,mdx}). |
| --media-include | Media glob when media is enabled. |
| --with-media | Include [media] in config. |
| --skip-build | Skip the initial index build. |
| --force | Overwrite generated files. |
| --interactive / --no-interactive | Control the setup wizard when stdin is a TTY. |
| --cwd | Run against another working directory. |
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: Examples
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
doctor

opendetail doctor runs a checklist and prints ✓ or ✗ for each item. It exits with an error if anything required is missing.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Usage

`opendetail doctor [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Expectations

Config: opendetail.toml (or --config) exists.Index: .opendetail/index.json (or configured output) exists.Route: the API route file exists at the configured path.Env: OPENAI_API_KEY is set where the route runs.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: Examples
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: The opendetail package is the engine: it reads opendetail.toml, builds .opendetail/index.json, and exposes createOpenDetail for answering from that index. The CLI (build, setup, doctor) lives in the same package — see CLI.
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
What ships in the package

| Area | Role |
| --- | --- |
| Build | Parse matched Markdown/MDX, chunk by headings, write the index (CLI or buildOpenDetailIndex). |
| Runtime | Load the index, lexical search, Responses API, stream NDJSON — Create an assistant. |
| CLI | Setup, index builds, doctor — CLI. |
| Config | opendetail.toml — Fields. |
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
When to use it directly

Use opendetail without Next or React for Node integrations (custom server, scripts, tests). For Next.js App Router, use opendetail-next; for browser UI, use opendetail-react.
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Minimal config

OpenDetail reads opendetail.toml at the project root.

`version = 1

[[content]]
include = ["content/**/*.{md,mdx}"]
exclude = []
public_path = "/docs"
`
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Fields

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| version | yes | Must be 1 |
| [[content]] | yes | One or more tables: each has include, exclude, and public_path for a Markdown/MDX root |
| media | no | Rules for mapping local media references to public URLs |
| fetch | no | Optional OpenAI Responses API tools: [fetch.file_search] and/or [fetch.web_search] |
| knowledge | no | Path to optional .opendetail/knowledge.toml sidecar with [[asset]] titles/summaries for images |
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Content roots

You can list multiple [[content]] blocks when docs, marketing, or other MDX trees live under different folders and are served at different URL prefixes. Each row is one indexed tree. Set public_path to the public URL prefix for that tree (where those pages appear in the browser), for example /docs for documentation and / for site-root marketing. OpenDetail joins each file’s route segments after that prefix to build citation URLs (and, beyond the first root, tags chunks as sourceKind: "page" for UI styling).
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Media

Use [media] when docs reference local images such as ./hero.png.

`[media]
include = ["content/**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,webp,avif,gif,svg}"]
exclude = []
public_path = "/content-media"
`

When media is configured:

http:// and https:// URLs are returned as-isroot-relative /... URLs are returned as-islocal relative paths are mapped only if they match [media].includeunsupported absolute schemes and protocol-relative URLs are ignored

If a doc references a local image that does not match the configured media globs, the build fails with a clear error.
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Fetch (OpenAI remote tools)

Optional Responses API tools — configure as [fetch.file_search] and/or [fetch.web_search] (native remote retrieval; no same-origin HTML scraping).

`[fetch.file_search]
vector_store_ids = ["vs_123"]
max_num_results = 8

[fetch.web_search]
allowed_domains = ["opendetail.dev", "platform.openai.com"]
search_context_size = "low"
`

file_search

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| vector_store_ids | yes | OpenAI vector store IDs |
| max_num_results | no | Limit from 1 to 50 |

web_search

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| allowed_domains | no | Restrict search to listed domains |
| search_context_size | no | low, medium, or high |

Remote citations appear in sources with kind: "remote".
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Knowledge sidecar

Optional knowledge = ".opendetail/knowledge.toml" with entries such as:

`[[asset]]
url = "/content-media/diagram.png"
title = "Pipeline overview"
summary = "Use when explaining the build step."
`
chunks.title: Core
Core

chunks.text:
Assistant instructions

The OpenAI Responses API separates instructions (how the model behaves)
from input (sources + question). OpenDetail merges harness and project
text into a single instructions string:

Harness (built-in): informational assistant behavior, grounding, citations, tools.Project (your files below): tone, priorities, product vocabulary — keep it short.

Loaded in order (project append):

assistantInstructions optionassistantInstructionsPath option.opendetail/OPENDETAIL.mdOPENDETAIL.md at project root

If none exist, only built-in harness instructions apply.
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Remote retrieval

Configure via fetch in createOpenDetail options or [fetch] in opendetail.toml (fetch.file_search, fetch.web_search). Runtime options override the artifact when provided.
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Defaults

| Setting | Default |
| --- | --- |
| model | gpt-5.4-mini |
| reasoningEffort | none |
| verbosity | low |
| store | false |
| promptCacheRetention | in-memory |
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Important options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| cwd | Working directory for config and instruction files |
| indexPath | Custom path to the built index |
| indexData | Preloaded index artifact |
| client | Reuse an existing OpenAI client |
| assistantInstructions | Inline project guidance |
| assistantInstructionsPath | Custom instructions path |
| promptCacheKey | Explicit cache key override |
| fetch | Override fetch from the index (OpenAI tools) |
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
Fallback behavior

If lexical retrieval matches no indexed chunks, OpenDetail sets fallback: true. The model may still answer using configured fetch tools; the flag means “no index hit,” not “no answer.” If the model returns no usable text:

`That isn't covered in the available sources yet.`
chunks.title: Core

chunks.text:
opendetail-fumadocs is optional glue for apps that already serve docs under predictable URLs (often Fumadocs-style /docs/... routes). It does not add a different assistant runtime: it supplies resolveSourceTarget helpers and a thin FumadocsAssistant wrapper around AssistantSidebar from opendetail-react, wired with renderNextSourceLink from opendetail-next.

For when to use this vs hand-wiring, see Get started → Packages.
chunks.title: Fumadocs

chunks.text:
API

resolveFumadocsSourceTarget — Given knownPageUrls and a source (kind: "local" or "remote"), returns { external, href } or null for unknown local paths.createFumadocsSourceTargetResolver(knownPageUrls) — Returns a ResolveAssistantSourceTarget you pass to AssistantSidebar as resolveSourceTarget.getFumadocsPageUrls(source) — Maps a Fumadocs-style source with getPages() to a flat list of URLs for the allowlist.
chunks.title: Fumadocs
Fumadocs
Fumadocs

chunks.text: OpenDetail turns the Markdown and MDX you already ship into grounded answers inside your product: same content your team edits, retrieved with citations instead of vibes. It is intentionally small — a build step, a local index file, a narrow HTTP contract, and thin adapters. Not a second CMS, not a generic chat platform.
chunks.title: Introduction

chunks.text:
How it fits together

| Stage | What happens |
| --- | --- |
| Build | Matched docs become .opendetail/index.json. |
| Request | Your route loads the index, runs lexical search, calls the Responses API with that context. |
| UI | Streamed NDJSON (text, sources, images) — render with your components or opendetail-react. |

Docs stay human-readable on the web; machine-readable routes (for example /llms.mdx/docs) remain available for tooling.
chunks.title: Introduction

chunks.text:
Who it is for

| Audience | Why |
| --- | --- |
| Product & devrel | In-app help without a separate knowledge base. |
| Engineering | Testable pipeline: artifact in git, predictable API, clear errors. |
| Agents | Stable URLs and structured retrieval from the same index. |
chunks.title: Introduction

chunks.text:
Packages

| Package | Role |
| --- | --- |
| opendetail | Build, core runtime, CLI. Start here for any integration. |
| opendetail-next | Next.js App Router route handler, NDJSON streaming, renderNextSourceLink. |
| opendetail-react | Browser UI — hook, streaming client, shell components, styling. |
| opendetail-fumadocs | Optional: known-page citation allowlist + FumadocsAssistant wrapper. |

Typical Next.js stack: opendetail + opendetail-next + opendetail-react.
chunks.title: Introduction

chunks.text: opendetail-next wraps the core runtime for self-hosted deployments on the Next.js App Router: a POST handler that validates the request body, streams NDJSON, and optional renderNextSourceLink for assistant citations in MDX.
chunks.title: Next.js
Next.js

chunks.text:
Request and response

The handler accepts POST with JSON { "question": string }. Malformed bodies return a JSON error.

Successful responses use application/x-ndjson; charset=utf-8. Event order and semantics match Get started (meta, sources, images, delta, done, error). Initialization failures before streaming return JSON instead of NDJSON.
chunks.title: Next.js

chunks.text:
Source links in MDX

For Fumadocs-style docs, pass renderNextSourceLink from opendetail-next into your assistant UI so local /docs/... citations use Next Link and external URLs stay safe:

`import { renderNextSourceLink } from "opendetail-next";
`

Use with resolveSourceTarget from opendetail-fumadocs when you need known-page URL allowlists.
chunks.title: Next.js

chunks.text: Follow this page in order once. After that, rebuild whenever docs change.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Install

Check the CLI resolves without a global install:
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Scaffold

Generate opendetail.toml, the default API route, and an initial index:

What that writes:

opendetail.toml — content globs and public path mappingsrc/app/api/opendetail/route.ts — the self-hosted route handler.opendetail/index.json — the built index (unless you pass --skip-build)

Add OPENAI_API_KEY to the environment your route runs in.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Config shape

`version = 1

[[content]]
include = ["content/**/*.{md,mdx}"]
exclude = []
public_path = "/docs"

[media]
include = ["content/**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,webp,avif,gif,svg}"]
exclude = []
public_path = "/content-media"
`

Tweak [[content]] globs and public_path so each root matches where those files are served. Add more [[content]] blocks when you index multiple trees (for example /docs and /). Full field reference: Core.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Rebuild when content changes

Run this in CI before deploy, or as a postbuild step. When something looks wrong:

Full command and flag reference: CLI.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text: Integration modes
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Self-hosted (default)

Your Next.js route, your Node runtime, your API key, your rebuilt index on deploy.

| Requirement | Notes |
| --- | --- |
| Node.js | 18+ |
| Runtime | Node.js (not Edge) for the default adapter |
| Secrets | OPENAI_API_KEY where the handler runs |
| Artifact | .opendetail/index.json after opendetail build |

Request body:

`{ "question": "How do I configure public_path?" }`

Response — application/x-ndjson; charset=utf-8, events in order:

| Event | Role |
| --- | --- |
| meta | Model metadata |
| sources | Matched chunks (and remote citations when configured) |
| images | Grounded image references from matched docs |
| delta | Streamed answer text |
| done | Final text |
| error | Safe public error payload |

Malformed bodies return a JSON error. Initialization failures before streaming starts return JSON instead of NDJSON. In production, repeated init failures are cached.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Hosted-shaped (scaffold only)

--integration hosted does not generate a route. It writes config and builds the index, but your app calls a remote endpoint you configure. The OpenDetail-managed cloud backend is not shipped yet.

Point the client at your endpoint:

Request and stream events are identical to self-hosted — no breaking migration later.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
How grounding works

Build time — Matched files are parsed, split into heading-based chunks, written to .opendetail/index.json. No vector database required.Request time — The runtime runs lexical search, sends the best chunks to the Responses API, optionally attaches remote tools from config.Instructions — The model answers from those sources, cites factual statements, avoids inventing coverage, and says so when a topic is not in the docs.

If nothing matches, fallback: true is set on the response. The model may still answer using configured fetch tools; if it returns no usable text, the user sees a clear "not documented yet" message. See fallback behavior.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
First request

With the default route running:

`{ "question": "How do I install this?" }`

Use useOpenDetail or createOpenDetailClient from opendetail-react when building UI — see React.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
FAQ

| Topic | Answer |
| --- | --- |
| No vector DB? | Correct — the default path uses lexical search against a local index file. |
| MDX with components? | Text and headings are the retrieval unit; runtime component rendering is not. |
| Hosted backend? | Client scaffolding exists; managed backend is not shipped in this phase. |
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text: Package: opendetail-react. For the Next.js route that serves the NDJSON stream, see opendetail-next. For the citation link allowlist and FumadocsAssistant, see opendetail-fumadocs.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Integration approaches

Pick the layer that matches how much UI you're building:

| Approach | Primary API | When to use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Hook + your layout | useOpenDetail | You control the entire shell and just need streaming state |
| HTTP client, no hook | createOpenDetailClient | Vanilla JS, custom stores, non-React surfaces |
| Shell preset | AssistantSidebar with connection | Drop-in column assistant; you supply navigation chrome |
| Citations as Next links | renderNextSourceLink | Local /docs/… citations should use next/link |
| Known-page allowlist | opendetail-fumadocs | You want to prevent citations from linking to unknown paths |

NDJSON route setup and Node requirements: opendetail-next.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Hook: useOpenDetail

useOpenDetail manages browser thread state and streams assistant responses from your POST /api/opendetail (or equivalent) route.
chunks.title: React
React

chunks.text:
Options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| endpoint | Shortcut for transport.endpoint (default /api/opendetail) |
| transport.endpoint | Request URL |
| transport.headers | Static headers or a function returning headers |
| transport.credentials | Fetch credentials mode |
| transport.fetch | Custom fetch implementation |
| persistence.key | Storage key for thread state |
| persistence.storage | local or session, default local |
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Returned state

messages, question, setQuestion, submit, stop, clearThread, status, and error fields: error, errorCode, errorRetryable, errorStatus.

Assistant messages track streamed text, sources, images, model, duration label, error metadata, and status: pending | streaming | complete | error.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Persistence

When persistence is enabled, thread state and draft input are restored on refresh. Interrupted streams are marked honestly after page reload.

Wire UI with Composer (requestState={status}), AssistantSidebar (connection={{ endpoint, persistence }}), or your own layout.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
HTTP client: createOpenDetailClient

Use createOpenDetailClient when you do not want the React hook — vanilla JS, a custom store, or non-React surfaces. It speaks the same POST body and application/x-ndjson stream.
chunks.title: React
React

chunks.text:
Client options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| endpoint | Request URL, default /api/opendetail |
| headers | Static or function returning headers |
| credentials | Fetch credentials mode |
| fetch | Custom fetch implementation |
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Request

`{ "question": "..." }`

Headers sent: accept: application/x-ndjson, application/json, content-type: application/json.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Status and events

Status follows idle → pending → streaming → idle or error.

Stream event types: meta, sources, images, delta, done, error.

Call stop() to abort the active request. Calling submit() while a request is active stops the previous one first.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
UI components

opendetail-react ships a layered set of React exports — use as much or as little as you need.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Layout

ConversationLayout — thread + composer regions; the structural foundation.AssistantSidebar — full column assistant; accepts connection (endpoint, persistence, sitePaths, transport) for wiring to the streaming hook.AssistantModal — same as AssistantSidebar but in a modal overlay.

AssistantSidebar and AssistantModal are recipes that compose the primitives below into a drop-in experience. You can also wire the primitives yourself inside your own shell.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Conversation UI

Thread — message list region.Composer — input area; accepts requestState to reflect streaming status.UserMessage — renders a user turn.AssistantMessage — renders a streamed assistant turn with text, sources, images, and error states.AssistantSources — citation list extracted from a message.AssistantStatus — thinking / error indicator (variant="thinking" or variant="error").
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Source link helpers

renderNextSourceLink (from opendetail-next) — renders local /docs/… citations as next/link and external URLs as plain anchors. Pass this alongside a resolveSourceTarget function into AssistantSidebar.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Styling

opendetail-react ships CSS for the default OpenDetail system and the Midnight and Signal themes.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Systems vs themes

A system is a complete UI identity: semantic class contract, layout, density, motion, and component anatomy. A theme is a palette layer on top of one system — same layout and components, different tokens.

New colors → use a theme.New chrome or structure → add a system.
chunks.title: React
React
React
React
React

chunks.text: Summaries align with the public changelog page. For full layout and dates, see the site changelog.
chunks.title: Changelog

headings: v0.5.0 — Docs index and streaming UX
chunks.text: v0.5.0 — Docs index and streaming UX
chunks.title: Changelog

headings: v0.5.0 — Docs index and streaming UX
chunks.text:
Build output

The CLI writes a single machine-readable index.json under .opendetail, so apps can load chunks for lexical search without a separate database. Paths stay stable across builds when content hashes do not change.
chunks.title: Changelog

headings: v0.5.0 — Docs index and streaming UX
chunks.text:
Assistant

The sidebar assistant accepts streamed NDJSON (text, sources, images) so the UI can render incrementally. Known doc URLs are passed through for safe source links in Fumadocs-style layouts.

Changes: Stricter validation for remote tool definitions; clearer errors when the index file is missing or out of date; minor CSS fixes for the input surface on small viewports.
chunks.title: Changelog

chunks.text:
v0.4.0 — CLI quickstart and configuration

opendetail setup scaffolds config, route, and initial index, with flags for base path, include globs, and optional media. opendetail doctor checks binary, config, and route wiring before you ship.

Changes: Configuration fields documented; improved progress output during opendetail build.
chunks.title: Changelog

chunks.text: OpenDetail — grounded answers from your own Markdown and MDX. Built for product teams who already ship docs and for developers who want a small, inspectable pipeline (local index file, narrow API, streaming events you can actually render).
chunks.title: OpenDetail

chunks.text:
In one sentence

Index docs at build time, answer from that index at request time, cite sources instead of guessing — same content humans read on the web, wired for your app and for agents that consume machine-readable routes.
chunks.title: OpenDetail

chunks.text:
Try the assistant here

Press ⌘ / Ctrl + J to open the sidebar and ask questions against this site's docs index.
chunks.title: OpenDetail
